meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: User Onboarding to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement professional user onboarding to overcome the 'no portfolio' gap. A step-by-step intermediate case study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: To overcome a lack of portfolio, build a high-fidelity user onboarding flow. By focusing on UI/UX, state management, and data-driven user journeys, you prove technical maturity. Using Antigravity and Claude Code, you can architect a complex, production-ready onboarding sequence that demonstrates deep engineering competence to potential employers.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a portfolio is the most daunting architecture a student faces. You have the code, but you lack the 'wow' factor that signals professional readiness to recruiters. Most junior developers build CRUD apps; you are going to build an experience.

User onboarding is the heartbeat of SaaS. It is where logic meets psychology. By mastering this, you aren't just writing functions; you are designing user retention. This case study bridges the gap between 'student project' and 'professional product' using the speed of Claude Code and the modular power of Antigravity.

steps.title: Architecting the State Machine
steps.description: Define the onboarding steps using a state machine to ensure users move linearly through the setup process.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React state machine configuration for a 4-step user onboarding flow including data persistence and validation handling.

steps.title: Interactive Progress Tracking
steps.description: Implement a visual progress indicator that updates based on the user's completion of onboarding tasks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable ProgressIndicator component that calculates completion percentage based on active onboarding steps.

steps.title: Optimistic UI Updates
steps.description: Improve perceived performance by updating the UI immediately while the backend processes data in the background.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement optimistic UI updates for the user profile creation step within the onboarding workflow.

steps.title: Form Validation & Error Handling
steps.description: Add robust Zod schema validation to ensure data integrity during the onboarding process.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write Zod validation schemas for a multi-step onboarding form with clear error messaging for each field.

steps.title: Finalizing the User Journey
steps.description: Connect the final onboarding step to a post-onboarding dashboard redirect and fire analytics events.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a final completion handler that redirects the user to the dashboard and triggers a success analytics event.

pro_tips: Always include a 'Skip' button. Forcing users through onboarding without an exit path is a UX anti-pattern.
Use local storage to persist onboarding state so users can resume after a refresh.
Add 'skeleton screens' during data loading phases to keep the user engaged while the backend processes.
Record the onboarding flow as a 30-second screen capture for your portfolio landing page.
Implement 'feature gating'—only show specific onboarding steps based on the user's persona selected in step one.
content.next_steps: After completing this, wrap your project in a clean README that explains your architectural choices. Link your live demo and share it on LinkedIn tagging the AI Builder community.
content.conversion_block: You now have a feature that differentiates your portfolio from 99% of other applicants. Don't stop here; iterate on your UI and build the next module. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
